title: licaria cogolloi
intro: Licaria cogolloi, also known as the "Quina de la Montaña," is a plant species native to the cloud forests of Costa Rica. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and digestive issues, due to its high concentration of alkaloids, including quinovic acid. The health benefits of Licaria cogolloi include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and improve digestion, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health concerns. Herbal preparations of Licaria cogolloi typically involve boiling the roots and leaves to create a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
